findings.summary: I2C false positive: +3.3V power rail detected as SCL line
findings.details: The bus_analysis.i2c section reports the '+3.3V' net as an SCL line with 5 devices listed as U2 (the GL850G), all repeated. There is no SCL net or I2C label anywhere in the source schematic. The GL850G is a USB hub IC and has no I2C interface. The +3.3V net name appears to have triggered the SCL heuristic incorrectly.

findings.summary: ESP32-WROOM-32 WiFi module incorrectly classified as Ethernet PHY
findings.details: The signal_analysis.ethernet_interfaces list contains one entry with phy_reference='U6', phy_value='ESP32-WROOM-32', phy_lib_id='esp32_physlayout:ESP32-WROOM-32'. The ESP32-WROOM-32 is a WiFi/Bluetooth SoC module, not an Ethernet PHY. This is a false positive likely caused by the lib_id containing 'physlayout' triggering a PHY detection pattern. The design has no Ethernet magnetics or RJ45 connectors to support the detection.

findings.summary: H-bridge with 4 power MOSFETs only produces 1 half-bridge detection instead of full H-bridge
findings.details: The design has 4 power MOSFETs: Q4 and Q7 (IRF9540N, P-channel) and Q5, Q6, Q8 (IRF540N, N-channel), forming complementary pairs for an H-bridge motor driver. The bridge_circuits section reports only a single half_bridge entry pairing Q6+Q5 with the output net '__unnamed_3'. The remaining power MOSFETs Q4, Q7, Q8 are not paired into bridge structures. The design should yield at least one full H-bridge (two half-bridges with a shared motor output). The power_net is listed as 'GND' and ground_net as 'OUT1', suggesting the high/low rail assignments may be swapped in detection logic.

findings.summary: GL850G USB hub differential pairs detected generically but USB bus type not identified
findings.details: The design has 5 USB differential pairs (U0+/U0- through U4+/U4-) connecting to the GL850G USB 2.0 hub IC. The differential_pairs section correctly identifies these as differential pairs, but classifies them with type='differential' rather than 'usb'. No USB interface detection appears in signal_analysis. The analyzer's USB detection heuristics apparently do not recognize the GL850G as a USB hub, and the U0+-style naming convention for USB ports on this hub is not matched by USB detection patterns.

findings.summary: I2C bus with 4 devices correctly detected with missing pull-up flagged
findings.details: The nep_hackerboard has a multi-device I2C bus (SDA/SCL) connecting U1 through U4. The analyzer correctly identifies both SDA and SCL nets in bus_analysis.i2c, lists all 4 devices per line, and correctly reports has_pull_up=false. The corresponding design_observations also flag the missing pull-up resistors, providing useful actionable feedback for this design.
